name: companion
description: A disciplined AI pair-programming companion: one steering document (STEERING.md — how Claude queues work, decides with brutal-honest recommendations against a requirements ledger, and keeps the code clean) delivered once per session, plus a small enforced core — a PreToolUse secret-scan that blocks committed credentials, a clean-as-you-touch pass (format the edited file, surface its blast radius, flag over-budget size), two PreToolUse edit-gates (show a wireframe before a visual change; present parked decisions when you return) with an advisory intent-vs-outcome reminder, cross-session task resume, its own task-queue CLI (`tq`; the companion owns the queue and deliberately does not use Claude Code's native tasks), an animated status line, and an on-demand /companion:audit. Replaces the earlier four-plugin system: the steering prose that was scattered across ~15 hooks now lives in one readable file, and only the behavior that must execute or block remains as code.
